acceptance_criteria.text: Deleting a key removes it from lookups.
Tree remains internally consistent (no orphaned child pointers) across interleaved insert/delete sequences.

derived_scenarios.text: Delete from empty tree (not-found, no panic)
Delete a key that doesn't exist from a non-empty tree (not-found)
Delete down to a single leaf (no rebalancing needed)
Delete triggering at least one leaf merge/redistribute
Delete triggering at least one internal-node merge/redistribute
Full integration: after inserts+deletes, remaining keys found via Lookup with correct fileID, deleted keys not-found
Empty-tree-after-delete convention (deleting last key of a single-leaf tree)

self_consistency_checks.notes: All prior tests from 1.2.1/1.2.2/1.2.3 plus new 1.2.4 tests pass, no regressions.
Confirms the issue's literal -run TestDelete command exercises real coverage, not zero tests.
